stem: A release enters the software release pipeline. The probability of passing code review is 86%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing unit testing is between 62% and 80% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing integration testing is 80%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing deployment is 83%. What is the overall probability that the release is deployed without incidents?

ground_truth.answer: P(deployed without incidents) ranges from 0.354 to 0.457 as the unit testing pass rate varies from 62% to 80%. Point estimate: 0.405.

solution_steps: The release must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the unit testing stage creates a 10.3 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the unit testing rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
